raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.3,"R1.3":0.0,"R1.4":1.0,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.0,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.4","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The delegation asks for positioning, which centrally involves shaping the thesis, gap, and contribution claim relative to surrounding work.","R1.4":"Assessing the delta from prior work is load-bearing because prior-work positioning fails if novelty is misstated.","R1.7":"The task centrally depends on relevant literature coverage and attribution, even though the main emphasis is contribution positioning rather than a full scholarship audit.","RX.1":"Uncertainty must be disclosed because claims such as firstness and nearest-prior-work comparisons may depend on incomplete literature knowledge.","RX.2":"Overclaim avoidance is active because the artifact makes strong novelty and first-design claims against prior work.","RX.3":"Scope adherence is active to keep the projection limited to responsibility assignment rather than performing the positioning edit.","RX.4":"Downstream-harm avoidance is active because overstated scholarship claims can mislead reviewers and distort academic attribution.","RX.5":"Provenance and traceability are active because prior-work positioning should remain tied to identifiable claims and sources."}}

rationale.R1.1: The delegation asks for positioning, which centrally involves shaping the thesis, gap, and contribution claim relative to surrounding work.
rationale.R1.4: Assessing the delta from prior work is load-bearing because prior-work positioning fails if novelty is misstated.
rationale.R1.7: The task centrally depends on relevant literature coverage and attribution, even though the main emphasis is contribution positioning rather than a full scholarship audit.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty must be disclosed because claims such as firstness and nearest-prior-work comparisons may depend on incomplete literature knowledge.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is active because the artifact makes strong novelty and first-design claims against prior work.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence is active to keep the projection limited to responsibility assignment rather than performing the positioning edit.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is active because overstated scholarship claims can mislead reviewers and distort academic attribution.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability are active because prior-work positioning should remain tied to identifiable claims and sources.
